LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT RITMO LATINO WIRELESS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH HUNT ARGUES THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR HIS NEGLIGENCE CLAIM WAS EQUITABLY TOLLED BECAUSE RITMO HAD KNOWLEDGE OF MR. HUNT'S CLAIMS BEFORE THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAD RUN BECAUSE DEFENDANT EDWARD LITKE FILED A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST RITMO FOR INDEMNITY AND RITMO FILED A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST MR. LITKE AND MR. HUNT. HOWEVER, THE SITUATION MR. HUNT DESCRIBES IS NOT ONE OF THE LIMITED CIRCUMSTANCES WHERE EQUITABLE TOLLING HAS BEEN APPLIED IN CALIFORNIA. (AGUILERA V. HEIMAN (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 590, 598.) BECAUSE MR. HUNT HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO INVOKE EQUITABLE TOLLING AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR REASONABLY POSSIBLE THAT HE IS ABLE TO DO SO, RITMO'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF JOSEPH HUNT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT HEARING SET FOR APR-17-2017 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH HUNT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE PROPOSED NEW ALLEGATIONS AGAINST MARGHERITA LITKE. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IF ONLY AND IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE EQUITABLE TOLLING AGAINST RITMO. LEAVE TO AMEND IS ALSO GRANTED AS TO RITMO AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF UCL AND IF RITMO BELIEVES THAT THE CLAIM IS DEMURRABLE BECAUSE OF AN INSUFFICIENT ALLEGATION OF MONETARY RELIEF, IT SHOULD BRING A MOTION TO STRIKE NOT A DEMURRER. THE COURT GAVE PLAINTIFF 20 DAYS LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

Off Calendar Aug-03-2017 Continued To Oct-03-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jose Manuel De Castro, Esq. (310-270-9826) of DeCastro Law Group for Cross Defendantitmo Latino Wireless LLC through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT RITMO LATINO WIRELESS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the third cause of action is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff Joseph Hunt argues the statute of limitations for his negligence claim was equitably tolled because Ritmo had knowledge of Mr. Hunt's claims before the statute of limitations had run because defendant Edward Litke filed a cross-complaint against Ritmo for indemnity and Ritmo filed a cross-complaint against Mr. Litke and Mr. Hunt. However, the situation Mr. Hunt describes is not one of the limited circumstances where equitable tolling has been applied in California. (Aguilera v. Heiman (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 590, 598.) Because Mr. Hunt has not alleged facts sufficient to invoke equitable tolling and it does not appear reasonably possible that he is able to do so, Ritmo's demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2017 Continued To Aug-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Joseph Hunt'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL MCCLOSKEY OF HALLINAN & HALILINAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-863-1520 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BURKE HANSEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-863-1520 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSEPH A. SACRAMENTO OF THE SACRAMENTO AND FORAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-775-0891 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. DAVID G. LARMORE OF THE DECASTRO LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-310-270-9826 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT RITMO LATINO WIRELESS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Joseph Hunt's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted as to the proposed new allegations against Margherita Litke. Leave to amend is granted as to the 3rd cause of action for negligence if only and if Plaintiff can allege equitable tolling against RITMO. Leave to amend is also granted as to RITMO as to the 4th cause of action for violation of UCL and if RITMO believes that the claim is demurrable because of an insufficient allegation of monetary relief, it should bring a motion to strike not a demurrer. The court gave Plaintiff 20 days leave to file a First Amended Complaint. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
